description_long: This is a large, heavy-duty rotary machine gun with a multi-barrel design, reminiscent of a minigun or Gatling gun. The central body is robust and features a series of six barrels arranged in a circular pattern, with a prominent rotating mechanism at the front. Two large, curved red armor plates flank the central body, providing both protection and a distinctive visual profile. The rear section includes a cylindrical ammunition drum, secured with black straps, and a sturdy handle for maneuvering. Several exposed cables and hoses run along the sides, connecting the drum to the firing mechanism, and a secondary grip is mounted on the left side for additional control. The overall construction suggests a weapon designed for sustained, high-volume fire in urban combat scenarios.
description: A heavy, multi-barreled rotary machine gun with red armor plating, a large rear drum, and exposed cables and grips. Its robust design and prominent barrels make it suitable for intense urban firefights.
description_view_0: Front view showing the six-barrel rotary cluster and the curved red armor plates flanking the central body.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the ammunition drum, side handle, and the network of cables and hoses.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the drum, the back of the central body, and the attachment points for the straps.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the drum, the main handle, and the exposed mechanical components and piping.
